title: Stanley-Stembridge Conjecture
statement_text: The chromatic symmetric function of the incomparability graph of any $(3+1)$-free poset is $e$-positive.
context_text: Described as a central driving conjecture in the chromatic symmetric function literature. Guay-Paquet made substantial progress in 2013 by reducing it to showing $e$-positivity for unit interval graphs (incomparability graphs of posets that are simultaneously $(3+1)$-free and $(2+2)$-free). The present paper develops the chromatic $k$-multisymmetric function $X_k$ and shows that Guay-Paquet's reduction arises naturally from the structure of homogeneous pairs of cliques.
paper_contribution: Introduces the chromatic multisymmetric function $X_k$ and demonstrates that Guay-Paquet's reduction of the conjecture to unit interval graphs can be understood and generalised within the framework of homogeneous pairs of cliques, providing a systematic method for deriving further chromatic symmetric function relations of this type.
notes: PDF source — extracted text truncates mid-sentence in Section 3.2 and Section 6 ('further possible directions for research') is entirely absent; any open questions or conjectures stated by the paper authors in that section are not recoverable from the supplied text.
paper_title: Homogeneous Sets in Graphs and a Chromatic Multisymmetric Function
